analysis_result: ANOVA p = 2.685e-02 (F(6,100) = 2.502); Kruskal-Wallis p = 1.122e-02 as robustness check. Per-group LUMP means: G1=0.434 (n=7), G2=0.400 (n=8), G3=0.407 (n=29), G4=0.423 (n=20), G5=0.408 (n=10), G6=0.399 (n=12), G7=0.399 (n=21). LUMP computed on 7 of 44 standard LUMP CpGs retained in GSE207207.
denominator_used: N = 107 Toronto-cohort tumors with EPIC methylation data and G1-G7 label (G1=7, G2=8, G3=29, G4=20, G5=10, G6=12, G7=21); 37 of 44 standard LUMP probes were absent from GSE207207 after the depositors' preprocessing, so LUMP is averaged over the remaining 7 probes.
assumptions_made: Standard 44-CpG LUMP probe list from Aran et al 2015 Genome Biol; LUMP = mean(beta) / 0.85 clipped to [0,1]; averaging restricted to the 7 LUMP probes retained in the deposited GSE207207 matrix (37 were removed during the depositors' preprocessing); one-way ANOVA via scipy.stats.f_oneway across 7 groups G1-G7; methylation subgroup inferred by matching the 'Gn' substring in the Methylation_Group column to tolerate whitespace/dash variants; Tumor_ID column headers in the beta matrix used as the merge key to Tumor_ID in the clinical sheet (M1014 has no beta column, so G1 has n=7 rather than 8).
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
- Methylation beta matrix: GSE207207_average_beta_values.txt.gz (Illumina EPIC, GPL21145; ~771,133 probes x 108 samples after the depositors' preprocessing).
- Clinical data: 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx, 'Clinical Data' sheet, filtered to Methylation == 'X' -> 108 Toronto-cohort tumors with a Methylation_Group label.
- Merge key: Tumor_ID (clinical) == column header (beta matrix).

B. COHORT DEFINITION
- All 108 Toronto-cohort tumors with EPIC data and a G1-G7 methylation-group label.
- After merging LUMP scores with clinical group labels: N = 107.
  Per-group N: {'G1': 7, 'G2': 8, 'G3': 29, 'G4': 20, 'G5': 10, 'G6': 12, 'G7': 21}
- One clinical sample (M1014, G1 per group-code) has no column in the beta matrix, so the final analytic N is 107 (G1 has 7 rather than 8).

C. VARIABLE CODING
- LUMP (Leukocytes UnMethylation for Purity, Aran et al 2015, Genome Biol): mean beta over a 44-CpG leukocyte-hypomethylated probe set, divided by 0.85 (upper-bound normalization), clipped to [0, 1].
- LUMP probes requested: 44.
- LUMP probes recovered in GSE207207 matrix: 7 of 44. The 37 missing probes were removed during the depositors' preprocessing (likely detection-p / SNP / cross-reactive / sex-chromosome probe filtering), so the score is computed over the available 7 probes.
  Probes present: ['cg00776080', 'cg01243823', 'cg06068897', 'cg07080372', 'cg11715943', 'cg15289427', 'cg20295442']
- Methylation group coding: text label -> 'G1'..'G7' by matching the 'Gn' substring, to tolerate spacing variants such as 'Cutaneous  NF_G6', 'Cutaneous NF_G7', 'Benign_NF-G4'.

D. COMPUTATION
- For each sample s: LUMP_s = clip( mean_{p in LUMP_probes_found} beta[p, s] / 0.85, 0, 1 ).
- Per-group summary:
  G1: n=7, mean=0.4337, sd=0.0545, median=0.4148
  G2: n=8, mean=0.4000, sd=0.0437, median=0.3878
  G3: n=29, mean=0.4068, sd=0.0291, median=0.4021
  G4: n=20, mean=0.4232, sd=0.0222, median=0.4208
  G5: n=10, mean=0.4080, sd=0.0250, median=0.4060
  G6: n=12, mean=0.3989, sd=0.0169, median=0.3994
  G7: n=21, mean=0.3987, sd=0.0231, median=0.3986
- One-way ANOVA across 7 groups (scipy.stats.f_oneway):
  F(6, 100) = 2.5019, p = 0.0268529.
- Non-parametric sanity check (Kruskal-Wallis, scipy.stats.kruskal):
  H = 16.5202, p = 0.011218.

E. RESULT
- ANOVA p-value across G1-G7 (Toronto cohort) = 2.685e-02 (F = 2.502).

F. CAVEATS
- Only 7 of the 44 standard LUMP probes are retained in the publicly deposited GSE207207 beta matrix; 37 probes were removed during preprocessing. The LUMP score here is therefore a 7-probe approximation. If the paper computed LUMP from raw IDATs before probe filtering, their score would average more probes and could differ modestly in absolute value (but the ranking and relative group differences generally track closely).
- One-way ANOVA assumes normality and homoscedasticity; with small group Ns (G1=7, G2=8) the F-test is approximate. The Kruskal-Wallis p-value (0.0112) is provided as a robustness check and gives the same significance class.
- No Sentrix-batch or technical-covariate adjustment; the question specifies the figure's simple ANOVA.
- 0.85 divisor and [0, 1] clipping follow Aran et al 2015 exactly; no clipping was triggered (max LUMP was well below 1).
